title: pyrus incana
intro: Pyrus incana, also known as the wild pear or mountain pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Asia and Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pyrus incana include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations to capitalize on its therapeutic properties.
